page.title: Medicare Extra Help (Part D Low-Income Subsidy) in Utah {year} | Vernal Medicare
page.heading: Medicare Extra Help (Part D Low-Income Subsidy)
page.description: Extra Help lowers or eliminates your Medicare Part D premium, deductible, and drug copays. See {year} limits, who qualifies automatically, and how to apply — free help from a local Utah agent.
page.summary: Extra Help (the Part D Low-Income Subsidy) is a federal program that can wipe out your Part D premium and deductible and cap your drug copays at about {maxDrugCopay}. In {year}, resources must be under {resIndividual} (single) or {resCouple} (married). Many people who qualify never apply.

faqs.question: What does Extra Help pay for?
faqs.answer: Extra Help lowers or eliminates your Part D monthly premium and deductible and caps your prescription copays — as low as about {maxDrugCopay} per drug for people with the full subsidy. It works with your existing Part D or Medicare Advantage drug plan.

faqs.question: Who qualifies for Extra Help in {year}?
faqs.answer: It's based on limited income and resources. For {year}, countable resources must generally be below {resIndividual} for an individual or {resCouple} for a married couple. If you have Medicaid, SSI, or a Medicare Savings Program, you qualify for Extra Help automatically.

faqs.question: How do I apply?
faqs.answer: Apply for free through Social Security (online, by phone, or at a local office). There is never a fee to apply. We can help you complete it and check whether a Medicare Savings Program would qualify you automatically.

sources.title: Extra Help with Medicare prescription drug costs

nextSteps.when: If you already have Medicaid, SSI, or a Medicare Savings Program,
nextSteps.action: you qualify for Extra Help automatically — you don't need to apply separately. Call us to confirm your drug plan is applying it.

meta.ogTitle: Medicare Extra Help (Part D Low-Income Subsidy)
meta.ogDescription: Extra Help lowers or eliminates your Part D premium, deductible, and copays. {year} limits and how to apply.

body.introHtml: Prescription drugs are one of the biggest worries for people on Medicare — but if your income is limited, Extra Help (officially the Part D Low-Income Subsidy, or LIS) can dramatically reduce what you pay. It's one of the most valuable and most under-used Medicare programs.
coverItemsHtml: Part D premium — lowered or reduced to $0
Drug copays — capped at roughly {maxDrugCopay} per prescription for those with the full subsidy
No coverage gap ("donut hole") — Extra Help removes it
No late-enrollment penalty for Part D while you have it
body.layersHtml: It layers on top of the {year} Part D protections everyone gets — the {partDCap} annual out-of-pocket cap and the {insulinCap}/month insulin cap.
body.limitsHtml: Extra Help is based on limited income and resources. For {year}, your countable resources (savings, investments — not your home or car) must generally be below:
limitItemsHtml: {resCouple} for a married couple living together
body.limitsOutroHtml: Income limits also apply, and they're higher than many people expect — it's worth checking even if you think you earn too much.
body.autoHtml: If you have any of the following, you get Extra Help automatically — no separate application needed:
autoItemsHtml: A Medicare Savings Program (QMB, SLMB, or QI)
body.applyHtml: Apply for free through Social Security — online, by phone, or in person. There's never a fee. If you're a Uintah Basin resident, we can sit down and complete the application with you, and check whether a Medicare Savings Program would qualify you automatically. Not sure where to start? See the dual-eligible overview.
